name: Hustl and commercial macOS timelapse apps
summary: Hustl is the incumbent commercial native app: rewritten Hustl 2 in Swift, app-only capture, pause/resume, idle removal, up to 8K export, Setapp distribution. Screen Timelapse lite (App Store) adds smart recording, blacklist apps, multi-monitor, customizable intervals. Lapse (getlapseapp/BetaList) sells cross-platform menubar with format and speed controls. Commercial layer proves willingness to pay; reviews expose reliability gaps TryLapse can target.
key_findings: Hustl 2 claims minimal performance impact and M1 optimization; Setapp reviews report fullscreen capture bugs, dark overlay on non-recorded screens, export playback issues in Preview (VLC needed); Screen Timelapse lite emphasizes smart recording and blacklist; Lapse BetaList: instant download on stop, MP4/MKV/webm/AVI, 144p–8K
implications_for_trylapse: Differentiate on export correctness (Quick Look playable MP4), honest fullscreen/multi-monitor UX, no dark-overlay surprise; Consider Setapp/Mac App Store later — direct sale or freemium first; Idle detection and app-scoped capture are table stakes for paid tier
strengths: Established brand; Rich feature marketing; Distribution via Setapp
weaknesses: Documented export and fullscreen bugs in user reviews; Multi-screen timelapse limitations per reviews; Premium pricing opaque without public transparent tier on site scrape
metrics_or_signals: Hustl 2 announcement 2021-era rewrite; Setapp subscription bundle distribution
